column7: Typo – In standard Pāṇinian compound formation, the pūrvapada must be stripped of its declension and presented as the uninflected base stem. Therefore, the breakdown should strictly be "kumuda + abhikhyam" (which undergoes savarṇadīrgha sandhi to form kumudābhikhyam). The inclusion of the nominative masculine visarga (ḥ) to make kumudaḥ is grammatically incorrect for a compound's first member. This is almost certainly a digital indexing artifact where the system mistakenly pulled the termination from the masculine headword (kumudaḥ) located immediately above it in the list. You may consider this as a Global Mandate to the entire Etymological Breakdown of All Compounds listed in AP !

column5: n. 1. Liver; 2. a mole, a freckle; 3. a water-snake.
column6: n. Liver; m. 1. a mole, a freckle; 2. a water-snake.
column7: Typo – Please note that this is a suggested "Print Change", in order to state the correct Gender for the stated definitions. The Vaidya Standard Dictionary improperly blanketed meanings 2 and 3 under the neuter tag that technically only belonged to meaning 1. You may compare this Gender Change with Apte and other authorized lexicons. No one says Neuter Gender for "a mole" or "freckle" or "water-snake" !
